entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY ENHANCING TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Technologies promoted to improve soil nutrient levels and productivity

entities.name: WEST AFRICAN SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The geographical region where soil fertility enhancing technologies have been promoted

entities.name: QUALITATIVE APPROACH
entities.description: Research methodology used to explore farmers' beliefs and rationales

entities.name: FOCUS GROUP DISCUSSIONS
entities.description: A qualitative data collection method used to gather group perspectives

entities.name: OPEN-ENDED INTERVIEWS
entities.description: A qualitative data collection method used to gather detailed individual perspectives

entities.name: FIELD VISIT OBSERVATIONS
entities.description: A qualitative data collection method based on direct observation of farming practices

entities.name: ROCK PHOSPHATE APPLICATION
entities.description: A specific soil fertility enhancing technology involving the use of rock phosphate

entities.name: CROP RESIDUE
entities.description: The practice of returning crop remains to the soil to enhance fertility

entities.name: FARM YARD MANURE
entities.description: The application of animal manure to improve soil fertility

entities.name: CHEMICAL FERTILIZER
entities.description: The use of synthetic chemical inputs to enhance soil fertility

entities.name: CROP ROTATION
entities.description: The practice of growing different crops in sequence to maintain soil fertility

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY DECLINE
entities.description: The reduction in soil nutrients and quality over time

entities.name: LAND AND LABOUR RESOURCES
entities.description: The availability and policies governing land and labor that influence technology adoption

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: Concerns regarding the availability and access to food influencing adoption decisions

entities.name: PROFITABILITY
entities.description: The perceived financial gain associated with the use of soil fertility technologies

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The long-term environmental and agricultural viability of soil fertility technologies

entities.name: ACCESS TO INFORMATION
entities.description: The availability of knowledge and extension services regarding soil fertility technologies
